# Backward-Compatibility Checklist: PRSG-010 Harden the Hatch + O5 Monster Epics

**Purpose**: Validate that PRSG-010 preserves existing flat spec discovery, typed reviewability exceptions, PRSG-007/008/009 fixture behavior, and Claude/Codex mirror parity while adding final backstop, O5, contextual routing, and boilerplate cleanup behavior.
**Created**: 2026-06-11
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md) | [plan.md](../plan.md) | [data-model.md](../data-model.md) | [contracts/](../contracts/)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

**Depth**: Formal release gate
**Audience**: Maintainers, spec authors, and PR reviewers
**Focus**: Current flat `specs/*` assumptions keep working; existing typed exception fixtures still pass unless intentionally updated; PRSG-007/008/009 fixtures remain valid; Claude and Codex skill mirrors stay semantically equivalent.

## Flat Spec Discovery and Navigation

- [ ] CHK001 Are O5 requirements explicit that current flat `specs/*` scanning remains the authoritative discovery model? [Clarity, Spec §FR-012 §FR-013 §Assumptions]
- [ ] CHK002 Does the plan require a regression check proving existing flat spec index/MOC generation still works after O5 parent manifest support is added? [Resolved - Plan §Split Plan and §Backward Compatibility Guardrails now require a legacy/current flat `specs/*` scan regression using existing spec-index fixtures and byte-identical ordinary flat-spec behavior unless intentionally documented]
- [ ] CHK003 Are nested child paths prohibited without requiring existing validators to learn `specs/<parent>/<child>` traversal? [Consistency, Spec §FR-013, Plan §Constraints]
- [ ] CHK004 Are generated `SPEC-MOC.md` zones still owned by `generate-spec-index.sh` rather than O5-specific hand patches? [Compatibility, Spec §FR-012, Data Model §O5 Child Spec]
- [ ] CHK005 Does the plan keep O5 support inside status/scaffold/topology surfaces without changing archived-spec cleanup or active-spec directory conventions? [Scope, Plan §Declared File Operations]

## Typed Exception Continuity

- [ ] CHK006 Are the existing accepted exception classes preserved exactly as `refactor`, `infra`, and `upgrade`? [Compatibility, Spec §FR-003]
- [ ] CHK007 Does the spec make intentional provenance tightening clear without deleting the operator-owned typed exception mechanism? [Consistency, Spec §FR-003 §FR-004]
- [ ] CHK008 Does the plan require existing reviewability-gate typed exception fixtures to keep passing unless the fixture is intentionally updated for PRSG-010 provenance rules with a documented rationale? [Resolved - Plan §Split Plan and §Backward Compatibility Guardrails now require the current positive and negative typed-exception fixtures to keep passing, with generated-provenance or code-fence updates allowed only when the PRSG-010 provenance rule is named]
- [ ] CHK009 Are invalid class, mixed casing, trailing prose, generated-template, and code-fence exception-like cases specified as rejected without making valid CONTRACT Markdown exceptions ambiguous? [Clarity, Spec §Edge Cases §FR-003 §FR-004]
- [ ] CHK010 Does the review packet still require exception class, provenance, and gate result when an exception is honored? [Completeness, Spec §FR-009, Plan §PR Review Packet Source]

## PRSG-007/008/009 Regression Safety

- [ ] CHK011 Are PRSG-007 routing/sizing, PRSG-008 layer planning, and PRSG-009 multi-PR emission preserved as the normal split path? [Compatibility, Spec §Assumptions]
- [ ] CHK012 Does the re-slicing packet require machine-readable PRSG-007/008/009 context without redefining those tools' existing input/output contracts? [Consistency, Spec §FR-006, Data Model §Re-slicing Packet]
- [ ] CHK013 Does the plan explicitly require PRSG-007/008/009 Layer 4 dogfood and contract fixtures to remain valid after PRSG-010 changes, apart from documented intentional fixture updates? [Resolved - Plan §Testing, §Split Plan, and §Backward Compatibility Guardrails now require `test-atomicity-route.sh`, `test-plan-layers.sh`, and `test-multi-pr-emission.sh` compatibility evidence when PRSG-010 touches their inputs, outputs, contracts, or handoff wording]
- [ ] CHK014 Are PRSG-010 routing schema changes specified as additive extensions to PRSG-007 signal vocabulary rather than replacement output shape? [Compatibility, Spec §FR-022, Contract routing-decision.schema.json]
- [ ] CHK015 Do weak contextual-probe requirements preserve the existing conservative route when evidence is shallow, stale, fixture-only, or conflicting? [No Regression, Spec §FR-021, Plan §Split Plan]

## Claude/Codex Mirror Parity

- [ ] CHK016 Are every planned Claude skill/reference wording change paired with the corresponding Codex mirror file where one exists? [Traceability, Plan §Declared File Operations]
- [ ] CHK017 Does the plan require semantic parity checks for changed autopilot, scaffold, and status mirrors, not only structural validation? [Resolved - Plan §Split Plan and §Backward Compatibility Guardrails now require Layer 1 Codex parity checks plus PRSG-010 Layer 8 parity fixture/dry-run fields mapped to every changed autopilot, scaffold, and status mirror surface]
- [ ] CHK018 Are Codex-specific mirror file names, such as `post-implementation-codex.md` and `phase-execution-codex.md`, accounted for where Claude reference prose changes? [Coverage, Plan §Declared File Operations]
- [ ] CHK019 Are docs/template exception-education changes required to stay semantically equivalent across Claude and Codex while avoiding live valid pragma lines? [Compatibility, Spec §FR-004 §SC-008, Plan §Split Plan]
- [ ] CHK020 Does the split plan place mirror parity verification in the same review slice as the wording changes that require it? [Completeness, Plan §Split Plan]

## Verification Pass

- [ ] CHK021 Does the remediated plan require legacy/current flat spec scan regression coverage for O5-related changes? [Completeness, Plan §Split Plan]
- [ ] CHK022 Does the remediated plan require typed exception fixture preservation or documented intentional updates? [Completeness, Plan §Split Plan]
- [ ] CHK023 Does the remediated plan require PRSG-007/008/009 fixture and contract compatibility evidence after PRSG-010 changes? [Completeness, Plan §Split Plan]
- [ ] CHK024 Does the remediated plan require semantic parity evidence mapped to every changed Claude/Codex mirror surface? [Completeness, Plan §Split Plan]

## Notes

- Gap markers identify missing or under-specified requirements and contracts, not implementation failures.
